title: MovieMagic
description: MovieMagic was my way of making movie discovery feel like conversation instead of form-filling.

content.text: I was tired of endless scrolling and generic recommendations, so I built a chat-first movie finder.
People naturally ask for movies in sentences, not in strict filter combinations. I wanted the product to understand that style of intent.
I used deterministic pattern matching and intent routing instead of a large language model. That gave fast responses, no hallucinated titles, and easier debugging.
Predictable behavior beat flashy but unstable intelligence.
Person-name ambiguity caused wrong filmography results at first. I fixed it with popularity-based disambiguation and safer fallback messaging.
The chatbot did not need to be magical. It needed to be reliably useful.
I also added deduplication in result rendering and improved regional language filtering so recommendations felt cleaner and culturally relevant.
MovieMagic taught me that conversational UX is expectation design. Be clear about what the system can do, then execute that really well.

content.suggestion: User prompt transforms into typed response and poster cards with filtered recommendations
